reviews.review_text:
Me and my boyfriend stayed at Traveller’s Rest for 2 nights, everything was perfect!
Staff was really friendly and provided tips on what to do around, amenities were sufficient and clean and the place was absolutely gorgeous and peaceful! We did a bush walk both by day and night and the way the owners organized the place in a way to preserve the surrounding nature while giving the guests an outdoor experience in connection with the surroundings was amazing, we were really impressed. One of the most amazing and peaceful places we’ve ever visited 😊

Wunderschöner Spot mitten in der Natur. Sehr ruhig, toller Boardwalk, gute Lage um die Umgebung zu erkunden. Schöne und saubere Amenities und Camp Kitchen mit allem was man braucht. Außerdem äußerst hilfsbereite Hosts die bei Problemen Gold wert sind.

1st of all, they call themselves
ECO Friendly.
Our view from the tiny house was a pile of rubbish.
In the rubbish pile, you can see where they burn toxi plastics.
The fumes that come off burning plastics are not safe for wildlife or humans.
2. The tiny house is in a caravan and campsite.
The picture looks like you have privacy.
3.
The owner checks on you all the time and are very rude .
4 the place was not clean at all!!.
The bush is a beautiful place and then owners of there land treat it like their rubbish dump.
Then try and make money off people saying they are looking after the environment!
I wonder if the government is aware.

We had an unpowered tent site. The area is very big. No problem choosing an area to set up. Lots of grassed paddock. There was a barbeque table next to the pond. Very handy. I had to change the booking as we were a day late leaving brisbane. The rearrangement was no hassle at all. The amenities block is fairly new, hot water, clean toilets and showers and a washing up/sink area at the front with hot water. The road into town is quite close but the road noise was not a problem. There is an ad onsite that indicates you can stay for seven (7) days free, if you clean the amenities block for five (5) days.

Fire pit is free. They sell bags of firewood which is enough for two (2) nights.

I will stay here again. But another for EV drivers; the charging facilities in Agnes Waters, 1770 and surrounds are basically non-existent. I drove to Miriam Vale to fast charge. Most of an hour's drive! Got back to travellers rest with 85% charge. Someone told me Pavilions was a charge station, in 1770. It is not. Unless you want to wait 5 hours for an 80% charge.

Purchased a day pass to do nature walk through the property which I enjoyed immensely. Such beautiful, serene  surroundings. The owners were so lovely and very helpful with my enquiries. The amenities were spotless and from what i saw everything looked perfect for a place to stay amongst nature, which I'm hoping to do in the future. Highly recommend.

Liked: Beautiful big grass areas lots of room so your not on top of each other. lovely owners.

Disliked: Warm at best showers
Free timed showers lovely custodians great atmosphere

Refusing to refund site fees for a stay that was cut short by a natural disaster is a low act. Floods caused by a cyclone closed both roads out of Agnes Waters on my first night. The owner Mike & other locals said to leave town during a brief window when the road to Bundaberg was re-opened - (the road was later closed again for days as flood waters rose again).

I followed Mike's advice to escape while the road was open. I asked to be refunded the pre-paid fees for the unused part of my stay. I was told they do not issue refunds, but I could get a credit - which is no use to me as I won't be heading this way again.

Us Aussies support each other during natural disasters - we don't rip people off. It's sad to see profit and greed prioritised ahead of ethical behaviour. Very poor form and UnAustralian. Do not prepay any fees that you are not happy to lose if you experience a natural disaster, health or family emergency.

Der Campingplatz ist wunderschön gelegen im Nationalpark. Es gibt verschiedene Wanderwege durch die unberührte Natur, direkt vom Campingplatz erreichbar.
Der Beitzer hat uns freundlich empfangen und war sehr hilfsbereit. Bei dem gesamten Ambiente merkt man, dass dort viel Herzblut reingeflossen ist ! Auch die Sanitäranlagen haben uns überzeugt: sauber und gepflegt!
Eine große Empfehlung!

We booked the Rainbow Bee-Eater Nest site so we could be with friends on the one site. It is a great sized, powered site close to the amenities. We had two camper trailers, however you could easily fit more if you wanted. The site is close to the amenities block, which was spotless.
Travellers Rest Stop is close enough to Agnes/1770 for an easy drive to town and beaches, yet far enough away so you feel like you're in the middle of nowhere.  It ticked all our boxes and I would recommend it to anyone.

Had a lovely 5 nights here recently on a powered camp site.  The sites are so spacious!

Amenities were very clean and showers were great!

There may be some traffic noise from the highway but to be honest it didn't affect our stay!

If you are looking for a spacious peaceful place to stay then Travellers Rest is the place to stay.

Thank you again for a lovely few days of relaxation ☺️

Fantastic campsite in very beautiful surroundings. There are plenty of opportunities to follow the wildlife and nature up close. A guided tour of the area with Mick is highly recommended.||Only the best recommendations from here. It has been a pleasure to be here.

My Bird watching experience at Traveller’s Rest. The property is set up for nature lovers, with very knowledgeable park owners. With a great bird hide, extensive boardwalk and marked cleared trails through out the property makes all parts accessible. Over 100 different species have been sighted on this one property and over 200 in the local area. Various options available to bird on this property.
I was really amazed with this place! A great place to camp with or without power. I can really see the hard work they've put into this place. Absolutely amazing place!

Great camp site with very welcoming owners, great amenities (toilet, shower, bbq area) and well located only 5 minute drive to agnes water beach. The only small downside is that the site is close to a road so it can be noisy when there is a bit of traffic.

Not the best time of the year to go ( rainy season) but it turned out to be excellent weather. This stay was 200% of what we hoped for. We choose 1770 travelrest because of the bushtalk of Mike but we didnt get ‘only’ this but we got the whole package: the breakfast with the first real live kangaroo view, the perfect spot for a campervan with power and water, the wildseat at the lake for the birdspotting at sundown, the love of a whole family for nature, drinks and medicine from nature!! |We would say Mike x Michy + childeren : you rock! We loved you ❤️ right away, you were one of the sweetest highlights of our 6 week trip down the eastcoast of beautiful Australia. |We would say when you’re traveling the eastcoast : stop@ travelrest indulge and then the rest…|( sorry: we had lots of photos but all were too big and we have no idea and time to make them smaller )|💋Two old and experienced travellers from Europe that don’t write reviews normaly
Excellent Customer Service, nice and comfortable place to stay, the amenities are in exceptional conditions.

Super gemütlicher Campingplatz. Kann man nur jeden weiterempfehlen!!
Wir wurden von Michi herzlichst empfangen und super nett behandelt.😌

Tolle Arbeit von Michi und ihrem Mann Mike.
Ganz ganz toller Ort! ❤️ Danke an Michi und ihren Mann für diesen ursprünglichen und gemütlichen Campingplatz in Mitten der Natur.  Beste Grüße aus Bayern! 🌞
Great place to camp, except for the main road is right next to the camping areas. So not much peace and quiet. It's got beautiful walking tracks and plenty of bird life.

I had booked and paid for another campground but when I saw that I was jostling elbows with neighbouring sites, I left. Google Maps showed me this place, and, if I'd known it was so good, I would have booked here first!

Plenty of room, great facilities and great people running it! Highly recommended.

Staying here is a must! Stopped off for one night, as we had to leave early in the morning to continue our adventure- Mike is such a beautiful man. He was so helpful in giving us directions (we got lost in the dark and ended up in the middle of the woods down the road!!). His personality and energy is so contagious, we truly felt so welcome to his little paradise plot on arrival.

Before we left, we went for a morning stroll with a cup of coffee on the boardwalk that they’ve built through the bush, listening to the bird song and reading all the little signs with interesting facts about the land, wildlife and flora that they’ve placed.

You could easily stay a few days here, there seemed so much to do. The toilet and washing facilities were spotless (beautiful hot showers!) as were the BBQ and picnic area.

Highly, highly recommend.
Great bush style camping excellent clean facilities and great bush board walk

I have recently just spent two weeks at travellers rest and to say it felt like home the moment i entered the property is an understatement. I helped out cleaning the amenities blocks whilst i was there and got to experience so many of the beautiful things this site has to offer. I left feeling like there was still more to see!
When i arrived i was greeted by Mike and informed about a talk he was hosting that night around the topic of bush tucker. During my stay i attended a few screenings about multiple different topics, history and storytelling, not to mention mike does “bushtracks” a guided tour through the property (which is a must do!) and also a forest bathing walk.
This campsite is more then just that, there is a boardwalk that leads you into the bush and offers up so many sites to see, different trees and plants, the forest actually changes halfway! There’s a fire trail which is like a boulevard you see pictured in magazines and calendars. I woke up to kangaroos grazing in the mornings and heard owls and possums of a night, not to mention the GreenTree frogs that like the bathrooms of a nighttime.
The family were so accomodating during my stay, making sure regularly that i had everything i needed. The children are so friendly and curious and both mike and Mickey are an absolute pleasure to communicate with and get to know.
Reception is patchy but accessible out there, one of the perks of the place is being able to switch off and immerse yourself with your surroundings.
There is a beautiful pond full of Lillie’s and lotus flowers, the bbq area is so spacious and town is just a 5-10 minute drive. The showers are nice and hot and the block is always clean.
This site really was a true gift and experience for me on my journey and I recommend it to anyone and everyone i meet
Thank you to you all, i cannot wait to visit again someday
Great location for an overnighter

This is the best campground we’ve stayed at so far. The owner Mike is so lovely and welcoming. At night he does a slide show presentation to give information about the surroundings which is very informative and entertaining. The campsite itself is picturesque and lots of wildlife. There is also a bush trail which has a boardwalk so very easy to navigate. The amenities were very clean and the showers were always lovely and hot. Couldn’t recommend this place enough. Would love to stay here again.
No telstra phone reception, lots of road noise

Great little camp just 15 minutes from Agness Water and 1770. Awesome spacious camp. Great showers & toilets. Even a boardwalk through natural vegetation with great interactive billboards for the kids. Great spot for a few days.
The campsite is located near Agnes Water. The campsite is very spacious and lovingly maintained. We thoroughly enjoyed our two nights there and can recommend the campsite :-)

Love staying there.  So peaceful,  clean and beautiful.
And beautiful boardwalk.
And only charge per person for an unpowered site, so is good for solo travellers
Great campsite, really quiet, with really good showers, owner was really friendly and location is 10 minutes drive from 1770

Arrived at 5.30pm in the dark.. was quite difficult to find our powered site in the dark. All are named but the names are at the back of the sites so are difficult to find in the dark. Spotless showers. Did the beautiful boardwalk before we departed in the morning. Very well done.
beautiful family that owns this place, definitely stay for a little while !

This caravan and camping paradise is honestly the most amazingly peaceful and beautiful place I've ever stayed at, the family are incredibly welcoming and great to chat and have a laugh with, you feel at absolute peace here !
Thank you to Mike and the family, we'll be back for sure 🙏

Great place to stay out of the business of Agnes Water caravan parks yet close enough to enjoy what the area has to offer.

Closer to nature.

This campgroubd has been the highlight of our trip. Big camping areas away from others. Great service and very relaxed few days. You can walk around a board walk and soak up the scenery. We loved it and will be back very soon 😍
No water or sink and taps in kitchen
A family-run, gorgeous campground in Agnes Water.  Two very passionate owners are educating travellers on preserving bushland and its habitats for little and big creatures. Highly recommend 👌
Absolutely amazing place with very knowledgeable and kind owners. Can't wait to go back!
Nice little camp ground near 1770, it has camp toilets and showers which were clean. The nearby boardwalk is good for a short stroll.

Amenities were very clean and the non-powered site was grassy and level - plenty of room with lots of space between other campsites. BBQ area modern and clean with large tables to sit at. The manager was friendly and he did an informative/interesting slide show at dusk. The one we saw was about shipwrecks that had occurred in the past. The board walk was an easy walk and the 1.6k loop pretty. It was nice to be out of town and in the bush.

We stayed at 1770 Travellers Rest for a week and really enjoyed our time there. The sites are spacious, level, and easy to access. The owners were warm and welcoming, and Mike’s knowledge of Australia and the local area is genuine and insightful. He shares great stories on select evenings, which added something special to the stay. Highly recommend this peaceful and well-run spot!
Love the serenity rustic charm. Such clean amenities and large sites. The board walk was great and educational

Michael, the owner of Bush Track Eco Tours made this Bush Track Eco Tour so interesting.  He has a wealth of knowledge which he imparts so easily.  It is an easy walk through his bush property on a board walk to keep your feet dry. The bird sounds were prolific.  We checked into his evening slide show the night before as well and now know so much more about bush tucker, native bees and what you can do with the paper bark from the Melaleuca tree.
Fantastic facilities, location is convenient located just far enough away to be perfect would highly recommend staying here.

Have stayed here twice, most recently at Easter and have to say it is an awesome place to stay. The amenities are kept very clean and the property just makes you feel relaxed. Big sites which make it easy to get into. Would highly recommend if you want to stay in nature with some modern conveniences.
Spent some time here back in 2023 and it was amazing! Great campsite!

After three weeks on the road, Traveler’s Rest 1770 has easily been our favorite stop so far. From the moment we arrived—greeted by a few curious kangaroos—we knew this place was something special. I’ve truly never seen so many birds in one location in my life. It was breathtaking. The sounds, the colors, the sheer variety—it felt like nature was welcoming us in.

What really stood out was the care. The caretaker was incredibly kind and generous, and it’s clear how much love goes into maintaining this place. Everything was spotless, thoughtfully laid out, and surprisingly affordable. You can feel the years of attention and effort that have gone into restoring and protecting the land. Staying here wasn’t just a stop—it was an experience. A rare treat to witness what it looks like when someone takes genuine care of nature.

We had a group of almost 20 ,  and still had plenty of space to enjoy the atmosphere ☺️. The Rest is set in a perfect spot to be close enough to town for quick trips for supplies & still far enough away to enjoy the tranquility & stars at night.
The host family are amazing & very thoughtful & accommodating from check in to checkout!     Providing fire pits & stocking firewood for purchase 👌🏻
The boardwalk is stunning & nightly nature talks & stories are informative & enjoyable for all ages.
The surrounding area has so much on offer & so much to explore!  We visited local businesses (ok ,we visited pubs🤣)
We saw lots of local beaches & explored some local 4x4 tracks which were great!    Local shops had everything we could possibly need on hand.
We all had a fabulous Easter break & will definitely be back!  I highly recommend giving Traveller’s Rest 1770 a go!  You won’t be disappointed ❤️✌🏻

Stayed there at Easter with my daughter. Great place good hosts. Took advantage of pay for 3 get the forth free. My other daughters turned up for two nights only $15 a night for them bargain. Good clean amenities and camp kitchen.

If you are looking for a camp to stay close to some attractions I will let you know that this camp itself is an amazing attraction!
There is a lovely bush walk track, perfect for those who love nature.
All the amenities were very new and spotless.
Really enjoyed my stay there!
Loved our stay here. Amenities are top notch, and the kids absolutely loved the board walk
Good location not too far from the town, lovely owners (or friends of) and great communication

Such a beautiful spot to camp for the night, spent 2 nights here, 10 min drive to Agnes, so much wildlife and facilities where kept clean, super quite campsite nestled away in the forest, so cheap to !!!

새가많고 야생 캥거루를 본장소
캠핑장내 키친 없음
식수있음 . 조용하고 아늑한느낌

Out of town camping
Hv hot showers and camp kitchen
Set in a bushland setting

The site itself is average. There's no kitchen facilities/BBQ yet, I suspect they're still building them. The sites themselves aren't outlined. The road into the sites are very uneven. The toilet facilities are nice and do seem to be cleaned to a good standard. Could really do with some lighting of some kind.

I'd personally say it was expensive (40aud) for something that is similar to a free camp site.

We booked in for 3 nights, got the 4th free (great deal at the moment) and stayed 2 weeks. Amenities are spotless, friendly service, pick your own camp site,  very pretty surrounds with an impressive board walk into the bush. Also enjoyed Mike's guided tour and night talks. A terrific place to base ourselves

Great quiet place. Great boardwalk and walking trail around the property.
Clean and tidy amenities.
No caravan water is available. Arrive with full tanks.

Spacious camp grounds.
Clean toilet/shower block.
Great bush boardwalk.

Went on the Bushland Boardwalk experience.  The guided tour was fantastic. Mike's knowledge of the fauna and flora was amazing.  Listening to the bush, you wouldn't know there was anyone else around for miles. The work still going into it, such as the nesting boxes and bbq   building, will only make the experience even better. We will be back.

My wife and I had a fantastic experience in this beautiful place! From the moment we arrived, we felt so welcomed. Having the choice of unpowered camp sites is a great idea, with plenty of great spots to camp. They have some wonderful walking tracks with lots of unique plants and wildlife.
Highly recommend!!

An excellent bush tucker and medicine tour.  Mike is a good communicater and has a vast range of knowledge. The landscape is sensational and the walk is flat and on boardwalk and wheelchair accessible. Mike also offers powered and non powered caravan sitesl

Had an amazing 2 nite stay, had the van parked right at the beautiful pond.
Lovely host,and great ammenities

Great stay - lovely named sites we were in Rufus whistler nest & sat out the wet weather. Great little boardwalk walk to explore.
Good showers & toilets.
Would return.

What a gem of a camp ground!  If you want to be amongst nature and birds then this is the place to be. A lovely relaxing environment with walks around the grounds.  Facilities were spotless, lots of bins and hot water for showers. Welcoming owners.  Highly recommend.

Spacious sites. Friendly hosts. Clean facilities and good quality drinking water.
Go with full water tanks and empty toilet cassette. A very pleasant change from overcrowded caravan parks with short boardwalk onsite. Access to disabled facilities needs improvement (concrete path).

Great hosts who care a lot for their place and their customers. They keep it affordable which is terrific for the budget traveller. The environment is beautiful and I stayed 10 days because I didn’t want to leave.

Really nice spot, plenty of room, clean amenities,
Highly recommended
We had a lovely stay at Travelers Rest! Lots of space between sites and quite tranquil amongst nature! Very clean amenities too. Would recommend!

The most amazing 'campers rest" nestled amongst trees, shrub and wildlife!
We loved bush walking 🚶‍♂️ in the morning and afternoon. A great place to stay, lovely clean amenities. welcoming atmosphere beautiful service. Can't wait to come back!

Fabulous campsite! We will definitely stay again. The owners were so welcoming, accommodating and generous. There's an on-site walk and clean bathrooms. Very family friendly. We didn't stay long enough to enjoy their sessions on foraging for local herbs and ingredients but would love to do that another time.
Great place, big sites, lovely location to camp

Lovely spacious bush setting with large sites
and no crowding. It Includes a long board walk through the bush property with wallabies and many birds Friendly helpful owners and clean functional facilities. Terrific bush getaway.
Great stay and a great boardwalk experience.

Had a great afternoon on the “Bush tucker and medicine” walk and talk with owner Mike. He was extremely knowledgeable and friendly.
Great experience and highly recommend.
Local produce and teas to try at the end was a great finishing touch too!

Thank you guys!
Just loved it here.stayed a week. Good base to explore from.Quiet  Roomy sites especially for unpowered ones.Excellent ablutions. Up there with the best wildlife camp areas we've come across.

This gem is located just off the main road and yet is really quiet and peaceful. The hosts are friendly and will do everything they can to make your stay enjoyable. The showers and toilets are also spotless! Make sure you explore the boardwalk. I’ll be back!

What a pleasant place to stay. Very quiet and lots of bird life around. Close to Agnes Water and 1770. We did the guided bushwalk and it was fascinating. Mike was very knowledgeable, and it ended with a tasting of various "native treats". The amenities were fabulous. We enjoyed our stay there for 3 nights very much, and would recommend it.
Well maintained camp ground. Friendly staff, new and clean amenities. Plenty of sites powered and non powered. Lots of birds around and a nice board walk through the bush down the back.

Super süßer kleiner Campingplatz, der Familienbetrieben und schön in die Natur eingegliedert ist. Ich glaube ich hatte selten einen so ruhigen Platz zum Schlafen während meines Roadtrips. Man merkt da hat sich jemand Mühe gemacht! Auf jeden Fall mal vorbeischauen!

*** Mining Industry folks, check this place out. If you want to take the ute somewhere for your RnR for a camp, fish, and relaxing time out, this is a champion spot.

Great spot, excellent value, friendly service, safe, comfortable place to pull up and camp when in the area. Hosts provide clean amenities and interesting activities for those who want to get to know the place.  Sometimes the "yoof" get a little rowdy on weekends around their firepits (provided with wood for a laughable fee 👍) but will calm down if asked politely 😆. All in all, a wonderful little treasure if you were weary, dusty and saddlesore after retuning from five days out west.

Delightful camping area.|Very large sites.|Power hook up only.|Short walk to new toilet / showerblock.|5 min showers only, so everyone can have a warm shower. |No TV reception ( which was great)|Water tap at sink at shower block.|No other water is available.|So bring full tanks when you come.|No dump point, need to take to SES grounds in town, aprox 8 km away.|Excellent new supermarket/ bottle shop approx 5kms away.|Fantastic new  300÷ meter board walk, where you can see lots of wildlife. They will be extending this walk, also noted that it is wheel chair friendly.|Owners are a lovely, helpful young couple with their 3 little ones.||Will definitely come here again. |Thankyou for a relaxing stay.|M & M
Put this on your places to stay

Great location back from the main drag and very clean amenities, the owners have put a lot of effort into making this a good place to set up camp. Reasonably priced and friendly staff, as it was a quiet weeknight and had paid for an unpowered site we were able to pick pretty much wherever we wanted to camp. Highly recommend.

Mike and Michi were great hosts and always available for assistance. The boardwalk thru the forest was a treat, as were the amenities that were in fantastic condition and close by.

Agnes Waters is a few minutes' drive and easy to get to. The main road is close to the camp site, but we heard no road traffic at night.

The unpowered site we used was level and had a fair bit of grass. The ground was also hard enough to use screw in tent pegs even after a good amount of rain. Word of warning... sand flies, say no more. Be prepared for some ankle biters :) We didn't cover up and so paid the price.

Loved this quiet tranquil spot and will definitely use it again for our short weekend trips to Agnes.

Amazing bush tucker/bush medicine guided .tour..wonderful native foods and teas to taste. Campground is set in beautiful native bushland, very spacious and peaceful with very clean toilets and hot showers. Lots of walks to discover and an amazing bushland board walk.||Highly recommended.

Unfortunately my husband and I's stay got cut very short, due to an unpredicted storm which destroyed some of our camping equipment... however, Mike and Miche were quick to assist us in moving our remaining gear under a nearby shelter on the grounds. It was really disappointing that we couldn't stay for the remaining nights, as the campsite is a lovely spot and amenities are really well maintained!

Thank-you Mike and Miche for being such fabulous hosts! We highly recommend this spot to all travelling to the Agnes/1770 area! My husband and I are eagerly awaiting our new trip up, so we can stay here again.
$30 per couple, per night for a non-powered site. $50 powered. Plenty of room for all sleeping styles.  Under 20klms to 1770 so well worth not paying to be in the town itself.

Mike and Miche have done a very good job making this beautiful spot worth visiting 👍 With powered sites and nice and clean bath and toilets and very kind and helpful owners, we did not miss anything. We made a wrong booking and arrived a day too late, but there was no problem, they just let us stay another day.  The nature is close, there are many different  birds and even a wild kangaroo family, you can see if you are lucky. There is a boardwalk into the bush, don’t miss Mike’s Bushwalk tours, it is very interesting, he knows a lot about aboriginal nature medicine and traditions. Our best recommendation for a peaceful spot to rest 🙏
Awesome place . Great board walk tour . Very friendly hosts..

Great off grid location for camping. Toilets are in great condition, you can plug your devices as well. Mosquitoes will eat you alive if you dont bring repelent. Also, kangaroos might walking in, as well as bugs and frogs.
Highly recommended!
After traveling the country from Perth to Qld this was by far my favorite stop

Zeer mooie natuurcamping, met gastvrije mensen! Eenvoudig voor flora en fauna liefhebbers. Je ziet dat de natuur hun passie is, de eigenaar gaf een PowerPoint op lokatie welke leuk en leerzaam is. Verder rust en mooie vogels en kangoeroes op het terrein.  Mooie wandeling rondom de camping. Tevens op korte afstand van Agnes Water en 1770, absolute aanraders te bezoeken. Wij bleven langer dan gepland.
Awesome place to stay. Simple but easy.  A++
Absolutely loved this peaceful place and amazing owners clean bathrooms and nice showers!

Heerlijk en met visie van de eigenaar - soort boswachter - aangelegd terrein. Lekkere plek om een paar dagen bij te komen. Voor toeters, bellen en zwembad moet je 5 autominuten verder zijn. Kortom aanrader!

Camped in my small RV.  So close to Agnes Waters without all the hustle. So peaceful and with lovely clean amenities and helpful, friendly owners who care about the environment and offer bush info sessions.
I'll be back. Janettee
Stayed 7 nights on an unpowered site. Lots of space. Good amenities and fellow campers. No pets and no silly billy's which was a refreshing change. Would stay again.
Een geweldige plek, met zeer gastvrije eigenaars. Een bonus was, dat de eigenaar dia's vertoonde en er een mooi verhaal bij vertelde. Hij organiseert nl ook Bush Walks.
Stayed for 1 night, great people to deal with, bathroom and showers where very clean. Close to town, clean camp areas Would definitely stay again !!
Big enough to have a bit of privacy. Great evening talk by the owner. Relaxing and enough shade for comfort.  Would definitely go back.
Great spot in nature, big shady spaces with lots of trees. Very quiet and so affordable!
Stayed for two nights in a non powered site. Very friendly and helpful staff. Shower/toilet facilities excellent. Very quiet and peaceful . Approx 5min drive to Agnes Water. Highly recommended
Great park, lovely amenities. Great host, even gives free slide shows several evenings. Only $15 per head

Lovey quiet spot with lots of space for all kinds of camping.
Has very clean toilets and showers. The owners are very nice.

Wonderful 2 nights stay, very enjoyable and calm.
Clean ablution block, rental fire pit, a bit of wildlife and very silent nights.
I would definitely come back to this place.

Wonderful campground. So spacious, in a gorgeous setting and we woke up to amazing birdlife. You can tell that the owners have a genuine passion for the place as they've created a top site that makes you feel like you're miles out of town, but you have the convenience of being just up the road.
Thanks guys, we loved our stay!

Great camp with good clean amenities. Quiet location despite being beside the main road into Agnes Water. Heaps of spaces to camp and can even have an open fire pit. Lovely walks around the park. We had a non powered site and were able to camp anywhere on the property. We chose to be near the pond. Short drive to 1770 and shops at Agnes Water.
Just a lovely spot Mike was lovely will be staying there next time in Agnes Waters

Great location
Wonderful hosts
Friendly owners.   Good clean facilities.    Environmental and peaceful   Plenty of space between sites.  Bush walks

Beautiful spot 👌
Would of been nice to have access to camp kitchen. Facilities that were available were clean and presentable
Great Attitude of Owner and deserves his Tourism Awards .. this is so good and NOT your average Sardines Caravan Parks that charge ridiculous Fees

Very welcoming and nice host! Great amenities with new showers, toilets and drinking water.
Nice spot and fit my campervan nicely!!
Excellent unpowered site, spacious, grassed. Very clean and tidy facilities. Definitely will visit again.
A nice park, lots of different sites. Clean amenities block.
Easy to book online and very reasonably priced for unpowered site. Clean facilities. No phone data so that’s either a pro for some and con for some. Great place to stay!
Great facilities and amazing hosts, would recommend to anyone looking to camp in the area
Really beautiful campgrounds. Super friendly and helpful owners. Great price & clean modern facilities.
The place is spacious and tidy, and the owner is very welcoming. It’s a great deal for those who travel alone.

Super friendly hosts Mick and Michi made us feel very welcome. Unpowered sites are plentiful, either in the seclusion of bush or near the amenities. Only 3 powered sites at present but more being installed soon. Amenities very clean with good hot showers and hot water outside for washing dishes. Slide show Frid, Sat at 6pm with interesting bush tucker info.
Very peaceful camp at night with fire pits avail. A bit of road noise , but atmosphere makes up for that. Definitely a great stay. Forget crammed CP Well done 👏 Mick and Michi

We loved our week camping here. Kangaroos visited once. There is a bit of road noise but its quiet overnight. We camped in the far field. If we had been more concerned about road noise we would have camped further back from the road. There is a nice 40min walk on the property. Amenities are new but basic, but has all you need. Might be difficult getting a shower or using the camp kitchen sink when busy.
You can't get better value for money, very clean, tidy and roomy camp ground. Wish we had spent an extra night there.
We pulled in quite late so didn’t get to experience the place properly but the owners were easy to deal with and finding a spot was easy. Beautiful location with plenty of shade and clean amenities

We stayed 5 nights and this is a great little free range camp site where you can get some much needed space.

The amenities while basic are really good. Clean, hot shower with lots of water. Small so if it's busy maybe a bottle neck.

It's on the main road so the road noise can be annoying during the day. All quiet at night so that's good.

This place is a little piece of paradise.  We easily got our 22ft van into a quiet bush spot away from other campers. Nice hot showers and clean toilets and Mike and Michi are so welcoming and all round great people. Nice to be on such a great bush setting with space between us and other campers.

I always enjoy myself when staying at Traveller's Rest. A beautiful campground with great bush tracks to explore. Mike and Michi are very welcoming and the facilities are very well maintained. I would and have recommended a stay here anytime. Thank-you.

A great place to camp up. Yes, it’s out of town(8ks)but it’s quiet and very peaceful, really who needs noise and lights.

Fantastic owners Mikki and Mike, slide shows, new ammenities, oodles of room with bush camping. Walking paths around dam and beyond.

Limited power sites, no water(but they deliver) and no lights after 9? Great for stargazing.

We will be back next year as the area is a must.
Great location, friendly hosts. Highly recommend staying here.

We stayed here for 1 night on our way to Mackay from brissy.  I loved this place. We camped in a swag just 50 meters from the amenities.  (Showers and toilets) such a nice quiet campground. U are able to hire fire pits and purchase some fire wood which is always needed for camping. And lots of shady areas available.
Would definitely stay here again. Thanks Traveller's Rest 1770.

The owners were relaxed and so inviting. Ground was wet after rain but drained very quickly. The signs are a little hard to see and finding the office to check in could do with some more directions. Over all I would stay here again in a flash.
Beautiful place to camp. Informative & interesting bush tour.
